objectives: Evaluate test coverage and quality for recently modified test files
Assess roundtrip parse coverage across all message types
Evaluate scheme validation negative test cases
Identify gaps between testdata fixtures and what is actually tested
constraints: Focus on: cli_integration.rs, snapshots.rs, roundtrip_all.rs, translation.rs, cbpr.rs, fednow.rs, sepa.rs, validate.rs inline tests
important_facts: 13 generated schemas total: head x2, pacs x7, pain x3, camt x4

roundtrip_all.rs covers 10 schemas with identity roundtrip tests: pacs.002.001.14, pacs.004.001.11, pacs.009.001.10, pacs.028.001.05, pain.001.001.11, pain.002.001.13, pain.013.001.09, camt.053.001.11, camt.054.001.11, camt.056.001.11, camt.029.001.12
ZERO roundtrip tests for: head.001.001.02, head.001.001.04, pacs.008.001.08, pacs.008.001.10, pacs.008.001.13
pacs.008.001.13 is the primary schema (FedNow/SEPA/CBPR+ all target it) but has no roundtrip identity test
roundtrip_all.rs fixture parse tests cover all 11 xml/testdata fixtures except head and pacs.008 variants
MT940 roundtrip upgraded: now does field-level comparison (reference, account_id, statement_number, balances, statement_lines) — previous concern resolved
snapshot tests now cover 3 schemas: head.001.001.04, pacs.008.001.13, camt.053.001.11 (up from 1)
10 schemas still have no snapshot: head.001.001.02, pacs.002 x3, pacs.004, pacs.008.001.08, pacs.008.001.10, pacs.009, pacs.028, pain.001, pain.002, pain.013, camt.054, camt.056, camt.029
CLI tests have zero coverage of: --scheme flag, translate command, validate with SEPA/FedNow/CBPR+ scheme, malformed XML input, invalid scheme name
schemes.rs external tests have 38 total tests; typed path coverage is good for FedNow+SEPA+CBPR+ on pacs.008
NO typed path tests for pacs.002, pacs.004, pacs.009, or any pain/camt messages
sepa.rs inline tests cover amount boundary conditions well (at-max, just-under-max, exceeds-max, at-min, below-min)
fednow.rs inline tests cover UETR validity, amount parsing, custom max amount
cbpr.rs inline tests cover only: name check, supports_pacs008, unsupported_message, control_character, allowed_whitespace — missing: actual field-level negative tests inline
Translation testdata/translation/ still only has input.txt files — NO expected.xml or expected output files for golden-file testing
testdata/schemes/ has 7 fixture files but NO invalid fixtures for: SEPA missing names, FedNow zero amount, CBPR control chars, etc.
pacs_002_001_14_minimal.xml was NEWLY ADDED (in git status) — pacs.002 fixtures now exist
camt.053 roundtrip in roundtrip_all.rs uses empty stmt vec — no statement/entry/balance fields tested
camt.054 roundtrip uses empty ntfctn vec — no notification entries tested
validate.rs (CLI command) has no unit tests on resolve_scheme() function despite branching logic
helpers.rs has inline tests for iso_date_to_yymmdd, charges_to_code, party_to_party_id, account_to_cash_account
translation.rs covers: MT103->pacs008, MT202->pacs009, MT940->camt053 (both directions), warnings test, multi-tx warning, missing required fields error
No test for translation with optional fields absent (e.g., MT103 without :50K:, without :59:, without :70:)
decisions_made: MT940 roundtrip concern from prior audit is now resolved — field-level comparison implemented
Snapshot coverage is now acceptable for the 3 most important schemas; remaining 10 have low priority
pacs.008.001.13 roundtrip identity test gap is highest priority: it is the primary validated message type
artifacts: crates/mx20022-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs (15 tests; no --scheme, no translate, no invalid-scheme)
testdata/schemes/: 7 fixtures (3 valid + 4 invalid); no SEPA-name-missing, FedNow-zero-amount, CBPR-control-char fixtures
testdata/translation/: 3 input.txt only; no expected outputs
risks_issues: pacs.008.001.13 has no roundtrip identity test despite being the primary schema for all 3 scheme validators: moderate regression risk
CLI --scheme flag has zero integration test coverage: if resolve_scheme() logic changes, no test catches it
translate command has zero CLI integration test coverage
camt.053/camt.054 roundtrip tests use empty collections — structural serialization of statement/notification entries is untested
testdata/translation/ has no expected outputs — translation golden-file testing still impossible
10 schemas have no snapshot: codegen changes could silently alter their output

iteration.summary: Performed a full read of all 8 recently-modified test files plus supporting fixtures and validate source. The test suite has matured substantially since prior audits: MT940 roundtrip now does field-level comparison, snapshot coverage expanded to 3 schemas, pacs.002 fixtures were added, and typed validation path coverage is solid for the 3 scheme validators on pacs.008.001.13. However, pacs.008.001.13 itself has no roundtrip identity test in roundtrip_all.rs. The CLI test file has no coverage of --scheme, translate command, or invalid scheme names. Translation tests cover warnings and errors but miss optional-field-absent edge cases. All camt/pain roundtrip stubs use empty collection fields, providing minimal confidence in complex structure serialization.
outputs_produced: Full test coverage assessment report delivered in conversation
next_actions: Add pacs.008.001.13 roundtrip identity test to roundtrip_all.rs (highest priority — primary schema has no roundtrip test)
Add CLI integration tests for: validate --scheme fednow/sepa/cbpr, validate with invalid scheme name (UnknownScheme error path), translate command smoke test
Add camt.053 roundtrip with actual statement/entry/balance fields populated
Add camt.054 roundtrip with at least one notification entry
Add translation tests for absent optional fields: MT103 without :50K:, without :59: (bare beneficiary), without :70: (no remittance)
Create expected output files in testdata/translation/ for golden-file diff testing
Add SEPA negative fixture: missing-names (SEPA_DBTR_NM / SEPA_CDTR_NM)
Add cbpr.rs inline tests for field-level negative cases (missing UETR inline, missing debtor name inline)

mentions.relevance_note: Code quality review noted xml_scan heuristics and helper duplication — relevant to whether xml-path tests are trustworthy
